qa_pairs.question: How do you specify the path for sharing a collection or subdirectory in Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: The path is relative to the share. To share the entire collection, you specify `/`. If you want to share only a subdirectory, such as "Subdir-01", you can specify `/Subdir-01/` or use the "Browse" button to select it.

qa_pairs.question: What are the available options for sharing a Globus collection with others?
qa_pairs.answer: You are prompted to select whether to share with people via email, username, or group.

qa_pairs.question: When sharing with a "User" in Globus, what information can you provide?
qa_pairs.answer: You can provide an email address or search by name or by Globus username.

qa_pairs.question: When is it recommended to use an email address for sharing with a user in Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: Using an email address is a good choice if you do not know a person's username on Globus. It also allows you to share with people who do not currently have a Globus account, though they will need to create one to access your share.

qa_pairs.question: When is it best to use a Globus username for sharing with a user?
qa_pairs.answer: Using a Globus username is best if someone already has a Globus account, as it does not require any action on their part to be added to the share.

qa_pairs.question: How can you share a Globus collection with multiple people simultaneously?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the "Group" option to share with a number of people simultaneously. You can search by group name or UUID, though using the group's UUID is recommended to avoid ambiguity.

qa_pairs.question: How do you enable write permissions for a shared collection in Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable write permissions, click on the "write" checkbox in the form. Once the form is completed, click the "Add Permission" button. In the access list, you can also add or remove write permissions by clicking the checkbox next to the name under the "WRITE" column.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to remove read access for a shared Globus collection?
qa_pairs.answer: No, it is not possible to remove read access for a shared Globus collection.

qa_pairs.question: How do you remove users or groups from the list of people you are sharing a Globus collection with?
qa_pairs.answer: Deleting users or groups from the list is as simple as clicking the 'x' at the end of the line containing their information.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to remove a shared collection in Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: To remove a shared collection: click on "Collections" on the left side of the screen, then click on the "Shareable by You" tab. Next, click on the title of the "Shared Collection" you want to remove, then click the "Delete Collection" button on the right side of the screen, and finally, confirm deleting it by clicking on the red button.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to your files when you delete a shared Globus collection?
qa_pairs.answer: When a shared collection is deleted, your files will not be affected by this action, nor will those others may have uploaded.

qa_pairs.question: What are some important security considerations when sharing files through Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: When sharing files through Globus, ensure you have permission if you're not the owner, verify you're sharing with the intended people (email is recommended unless you have the exact account name), trust group owners if sharing with groups you don't control, maintain backups of important files if granting write access, and restrict sharing to a subdirectory rather than your top-level home directory.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to verify the identity of the person you add to an access list in Globus sharing?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important because people often have the same or similar names, and Globus usernames are not linked to Alliance usernames. The recommended method is to use the email address of the person you wish to share with, unless you have their exact account name.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended regarding backups when granting write access to a shared Globus collection?
qa_pairs.answer: If granting write access, it is highly recommended that you have backups of important files that are not on the shared endpoint, as users of the shared endpoint may delete or overwrite files.

qa_pairs.question: Why should Globus sharing be restricted to a subdirectory instead of a top-level home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: It is highly recommended that sharing be restricted to a subdirectory rather than your top-level home directory to enhance security and control.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary benefit of using Globus groups?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus groups provide an easy way to manage permissions for sharing with multiple users, allowing you to control access for several users simultaneously from the sharing interface.
